argument: The contested claim states that Devin Marsh served as a full-time CEO of DataLoom starting in March 2019 while simultaneously holding a full-time position as an Infrastructure Engineer at Stripe through mid-2020. The problem arises from the fact that these roles, as described, both require full-time commitment, making it operationally improbable for one person to legitimately fulfill both simultaneously. The source of this claim is primarily the founder's own seed deck [deck], which lacks independent verification. Furthermore, corroborating sources, such as Marsh's professional profile explicitly stating a full-time tenure at Stripe [web], challenge the dual full-time role assertion. Without evidence from independent or third-party documentation that could explain or justify this overlap, such as public filings or external acknowledgments, the claim remains unsupported by available evidence. Even allowing for creative interpretations, such as a time-split or advisory capacity, the claim as stated — two full-time positions — is either overstated or misleading.
